finding: Two agent sessions from Codex or another editor integration can operate through the same actor id and claim surface, making active-work warnings look like self-continuation instead of a distinct concurrent operator.
expectedBehavior: ATM should surface a distinct session, ticket, and editor identity for each operator instance and warn when another live session touches the same task or shared Git/index lane, even if actor id and task id match.
evidenceOrRepro: During TASK-RFT-0037, ATM surfaced active work and branch queue signals, but the shared codex-task-rft-0037 actor/claim made the other session indistinguishable until commit-status showed a separate in-flight owner PID and the user flagged the collision. During TASK-RFT-0038 closeout, Broker reported TASK-RFT-0038 and ATM-GOV-0146 as parallel-safe by file scope while pre-close still blocked on foreign staged governance and the branch commit queue owned by codex-gpt-5-5-captain, showing that cross-editor session identity must also cover shared index and queue resources.
followUp: Open a governance task to add editor-agnostic session-key or ticket-level identity to broker status, next active-work summaries, branch commit queue diagnostics, Git index lane diagnostics, and task claim warnings; include regressions where two sessions share actor/task and where distinct editor integrations share a branch/index but must be reported as distinct operators.
